data.content:
The file is generated successfully but never added to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's default `**/*.cs` glob expands at evaluation time — before any targets run — so the generated file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `Compile` and `FileWrites` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file during the build (before `CoreCompile`), but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's default glob for `Compile` items is evaluated at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, and also to `FileWrites` for clean support.

Let me use the skill for this since it's available.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's what was wrong and the fix:

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` during the build, but MSBuild's default `**/*.cs` glob expands at **evaluation time** — before any targets execute. So the generated file existed on disk but was never passed to the compiler.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target to explicitly include the generated file in:
- `Compile` — so the compiler sees it
- `FileWrites` — so `dotnet clean` removes it
